title: Declaring This New Years’ Season Mahanaim
summary: “And Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him. And when Jacob saw them, he said, This is God's host: and he called the name of that place Mahanaim.” Genesis 32:1-2 At this point in Jacob’s life, he had suffered at the hands of two people for 20 years: Esau and Laban. It was because of Esau that he was a refugee in Haran separated from his family and the promise of God in the Land of Canaan. In Haran, he was tormented by his uncle Laban. This is perhaps why Jacob accepted his sufferings in Haran for so long – he was too afraid to return to Canaan because of Esau. When we do not have the strength to return to where we should be in the Lord, thank God that he himself intervenes and pushes us towards his will and promises. God told Jacob: “And the LORD said unto Jacob, Return unto the land of thy fathers, and to thy kindred; and I will be with thee” (Gen 31:3). When Jacob decided to return to the land of promise, the Bible says that he saw two companies of angels – for that is what Mahanaim means: two camps of angels. Hallelujah! One camp of angels went behind him to close the door to Laban and the past 20 years, and another camp of angels to face Esau and the future. I want to call this time of the year, as we head to 2023, Mahanaim – the Lord Jesus will send two camps of angels: one to close the door on the pain and sufferings of 2022 and another to lead us to confront 2023. Praise Jes
